The email template’s default content, translated to the current user's language.

member.name: M:Okta.Sdk.Api.CustomizationApi.SendTestEmailAsync(System.String,System.String,System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Send a Test Email Sends a test email to the current user’s primary and secondary email addresses. The email content is selected based on the following priority: 1. The email customization for the language specified in the `language` query parameter. 2. The email template's default customization. 3. The email template’s default content, translated to the current user's language.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: The ID of the brand.
The name of the email template.
The language to use for the email. Defaults to the current user's language if unspecified. (optional)
Cancellation Token to cancel the request.

member.name: M:Okta.Sdk.Api.IDeviceApiAsync.ListDevices(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.remarks:
Lists all devices with pagination support. A subset of Devices can be returned that match a supported search criteria using the `search` query parameter. Searches for devices based on the properties specified in the `search` parameter conforming SCIM filter specifications (case-insensitive). This data is eventually consistent. The API returns different results depending on specified queries in the request. Empty list is returned if no objects match `search` request. > **Note:** Listing devices with `search` should not be used as a part of any critical flows—such as authentication or updates—to prevent potential data loss. `search` results may not reflect the latest information, as this endpoint uses a search index which may not be up-to-date with recent updates to the object.
Don't use search results directly for record updates, as the data might be stale and therefore overwrite newer data, resulting in data loss.
Use an `id` lookup for records that you update to ensure your results contain the latest data. This operation equires [URL encoding](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Percent-encoding). For example, `search=profile.displayName eq \"Bob\"` is encoded as `search=profile.displayName%20eq%20%22Bob%22`.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: The cursor to use for pagination. It is an opaque string that specifies your current location in the list and is obtained from the `Link` response header. See [Pagination](/#pagination) for more information. (optional)
A limit on the number of objects to return. (optional, default to 20)
SCIM filter expression that filters the results. Searches include all Device `profile` properties, as well as the Device `id`, `status` and `lastUpdated` properties. (optional)
Cancellation Token to cancel the request.

member.name: M:Okta.Sdk.Api.IDeviceApiAsync.ListDevicesWithHttpInfoAsync(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)

Empty list is returned if no objects match `search` request. > **Note:** Listing devices with `search` should not be used as a part of any critical flows—such as authentication or updates—to prevent potential data loss. `search` results may not reflect the latest information, as this endpoint uses a search index which may not be up-to-date with recent updates to the object.
Don't use search results directly for record updates, as the data might be stale and therefore overwrite newer data, resulting in data loss.
Use an `id` lookup for records that you update to ensure your results contain the latest data. This operation equires [URL encoding](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Percent-encoding). For example, `search=profile.displayName eq \"Bob\"` is encoded as `search=profile.displayName%20eq%20%22Bob%22`.

member.name: M:Okta.Sdk.Api.DeviceApi.ListDevicesWithHttpInfoAsync(System.String,System.Nullable{System.Int32},System.String,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary:
List all Devices Lists all devices with pagination support. A subset of Devices can be returned that match a supported search criteria using the `search` query parameter. Searches for devices based on the properties specified in the `search` parameter conforming SCIM filter specifications (case-insensitive). This data is eventually consistent. The API returns different results depending on specified queries in the request. Empty list is returned if no objects match `search` request. > **Note:** Listing devices with `search` should not be used as a part of any critical flows—such as authentication or updates—to prevent potential data loss. `search` results may not reflect the latest information, as this endpoint uses a search index which may not be up-to-date with recent updates to the object.
Don't use search results directly for record updates, as the data might be stale and therefore overwrite newer data, resulting in data loss.
Use an `id` lookup for records that you update to ensure your results contain the latest data. This operation equires [URL encoding](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Percent-encoding). For example, `search=profile.displayName eq \"Bob\"` is encoded as `search=profile.displayName%20eq%20%22Bob%22`.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: The cursor to use for pagination. It is an opaque string that specifies your current location in the list and is obtained from the `Link` response header. See [Pagination](/#pagination) for more information. (optional)
A limit on the number of objects to return. (optional, default to 20)
SCIM filter expression that filters the results. Searches include all Device `profile` properties, as well as the Device `id`, `status` and `lastUpdated` properties. (optional)
Cancellation Token to cancel the request.

member.name: M:Okta.Sdk.Api.IRiskEventApiAsync.SendRiskEventsAsync(System.Collections.Generic.List{Okta.Sdk.Model.RiskEvent},System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.remarks: A Risk Provider can send Risk Events to Okta using this API. This API has a rate limit of 30 requests per minute. The caller should include multiple Risk Events (up to a maximum of 20 events) in a single payload to reduce the number of API calls. If a client has more risk signals to send than what the API supports, we recommend prioritizing posting high risk signals.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: Cancellation Token to cancel the request.

member.name: M:Okta.Sdk.Api.ISchemaApiAsync.GetLogStreamSchemaAsync(Okta.Sdk.Model.LogStreamType,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Retrieve the Log Stream Schema for the schema type
member.remarks: Retrieves the schema for a Log Stream type. The `logStreamType` element in the URL specifies the Log Stream type, which is either `aws_eventbridge` or `splunk_cloud_logstreaming`. Use the `aws_eventbridge` literal to retrieve the AWS EventBridge type schema, and use the `splunk_cloud_logstreaming` literal retrieve the Splunk Cloud type schema.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: Cancellation Token to cancel the request.

member.name: M:Okta.Sdk.Api.SchemaApi.GetLogStreamSchemaWithHttpInfoAsync(Okta.Sdk.Model.LogStreamType,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Retrieve the Log Stream Schema for the schema type Retrieves the schema for a Log Stream type. The `logStreamType` element in the URL specifies the Log Stream type, which is either `aws_eventbridge` or `splunk_cloud_logstreaming`. Use the `aws_eventbridge` literal to retrieve the AWS EventBridge type schema, and use the `splunk_cloud_logstreaming` literal retrieve the Splunk Cloud type schema.

member.name: T:Okta.Sdk.Api.IUserApiAsync
member.summary: Represents a collection of functions to interact with the API endpoints

member.name: M:Okta.Sdk.Api.IUserApiAsync.ActivateUserAsync(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.remarks: Activates a user. This operation can only be performed on users with a `STAGED` status. Activation of a user is an asynchronous operation. The user will have the `transitioningToStatus` property with a value of `ACTIVE` during activation to indicate that the user hasn't completed the asynchronous operation. The user will have a status of `ACTIVE` when the activation process is complete.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: Sends an activation email to the user if true
Cancellation Token to cancel the request.

member.name: M:Okta.Sdk.Api.IUserApiAsync.ActivateUserWithHttpInfoAsync(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.remarks: Activates a user. This operation can only be performed on users with a `STAGED` status. Activation of a user is an asynchronous operation. The user will have the `transitioningToStatus` property with a value of `ACTIVE` during activation to indicate that the user hasn't completed the asynchronous operation. The user will have a status of `ACTIVE` when the activation process is complete.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call

member.name: M:Okta.Sdk.Api.UserApi.ActivateUserAsync(System.String,System.Boolean,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Activate a User Activates a user. This operation can only be performed on users with a `STAGED` status. Activation of a user is an asynchronous operation. The user will have the `transitioningToStatus` property with a value of `ACTIVE` during activation to indicate that the user hasn't completed the asynchronous operation. The user will have a status of `ACTIVE` when the activation process is complete.
member.exception: Thrown when fails to make API call
member.param: Sends an activation email to the user if true
Cancellation Token to cancel the request.

member.name: P:Okta.Sdk.Client.Configuration.DateTimeFormat
member.summary: Gets or sets the date time format used when serializing in the ApiClient By default, it's set to ISO 8601 - "o", for others see: https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/az4se3k1(v=vs.110).aspx and https://msdn.microsoft.com/en-us/library/8kb3ddd4(v=vs.110).aspx No validation is done to ensure that the string you're providing is valid

member.name: P:Okta.Sdk.Client.Configuration.ApiKeyPrefix
member.summary:
Gets or sets the prefix (e.g. Token) of the API key based on the authentication name.
Whatever you set here will be prepended to the value defined in AddApiKey.
An example invocation here might be:
ApiKeyPrefix["Authorization"] = "Bearer";
… where ApiKey["Authorization"] would then be used to set the value of your bearer token.
OAuth2 workflows should set tokens via AccessToken.
member.value: The prefix of the API key.

member.name: P:Okta.Sdk.Client.Configuration.ApiKey
member.summary: Gets or sets the API key based on the authentication name.
